variables.context: A text classification model deployed in production shows 95.2% accuracy on the holdout set, but p95 inference latency is 2.3 seconds per request, well above the 500ms SLA.
variables.criteria: Evaluate the tradeoff between model accuracy and inference latency. Recommend whether to keep, optimize, or replace the model.

variables.context: A summarization prompt tested against 50 documents produces factually incorrect claims in 18% of outputs. The hallucinations include invented statistics and misattributed quotes.
variables.criteria: Evaluate the hallucination rate and suggest prompt-level mitigations. Identify which hallucination categories are most dangerous.

variables.context: A/B test between model-v2.1 (fine-tuned on domain data) and model-v2.0 (base). v2.1 shows +3.8% relevance score improvement but -1.2% factual accuracy on the eval set. Traffic split is 50/50 over 10,000 requests.
variables.criteria: Evaluate whether the A/B test results are statistically significant and recommend which model version to promote to production.

variables.context: Fine-tuned Qwen 2.5 14B on 5,000 domain-specific Q&A pairs for 3 epochs. Base model scores 62% on domain eval set. Fine-tuned model scores 81% on domain eval but dropped from 74% to 68% on general benchmarks (MMLU subset).
variables.criteria: Evaluate the fine-tuning outcome. Assess whether the domain accuracy gain justifies the general capability regression. Recommend next steps.

variables.context: RAG pipeline using ChromaDB with 12,000 embedded documents. Retrieval recall@5 is 0.72, precision@5 is 0.45. End-to-end answer accuracy is 64%. Embedding model is all-MiniLM-L6-v2. Chunk size is 512 tokens with 50 token overlap.
variables.criteria: Evaluate the retrieval quality metrics and identify bottlenecks. Recommend specific improvements to chunking strategy, embedding model, or retrieval parameters.
